# feat(runtime): wire arm_time_limits into LocalRuntime + AsyncLocalRuntime (#912 Shard 6)

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `abdae68399bb` — feat(runtime): wire arm_time_limits into LocalRuntime + AsyncLocalRuntime (#912 Shard 6)

**Body**:

The /redteam Round 1 audit caught the HIGH spec-compliance gap: 5
in-process runtimes accepted soft_time_limit/time_limit kwargs but
ONLY called _validate_limits() and dropped them on the floor —
never armed arm_time_limits. The README quickstart::

    runtime = LocalRuntime()
    runtime.execute(workflow.build(), soft_time_limit=2)

silently never raised SoftTimeLimitExceeded. Same fake-dispatch
failure mode as zero-tolerance.md Rule 2 § "Fake dispatch": the
docstring promised the contract; the code did not deliver.

LocalRuntime.execute (sync entry point):
  - Layer a fresh CancellationToken under the user's so timer cancels
    don't poison the user's token state.
  - Arm threading.Timer-based deadlines around the inner _execute_sync
    / _execute_async dispatch.
  - Catch WorkflowCancelledError and classify into Soft/Hard via
    _TimeLimitClassifier.
  - Post-completion poll for hard_deadline_reached + soft-fired-but-
    workflow-completed cases (Shard 2 invariant 5).
  - Disarm in finally so the no-limit path stays allocation-free.

AsyncLocalRuntime.execute_workflow_async (async entry point):
  - Mirror the above using arm_time_limits_async (asyncio tasks on
    the same event loop).
  - Add typed Soft/HardTimeLimitExceeded re-raise above the broad
    `except Exception` so the typed exceptions propagate untouched
    instead of being swallowed and re-wrapped as
    WorkflowExecutionError.

Tier-2 tests exercise both paths against real runtime instances with
sleeping workflows; the validation tests confirm caller error raises
at the entry point per the Shard 6 finite-check hardening.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
